name: Software & AI Job Market
description: Demand, salary, sponsorship, and market size for software/AI engineers.

factors.label: General software-engineer demand & market depth
factors.description: Research official shortage/priority-occupation lists (Germany Fachkraefte, UK Immigration Salary List, Australia Skills Priority List, Canada Express Entry categories) and national ICT vacancy/employment data for whether software developer is in shortage. Anchors: ~90 = on shortage list, large absorptive market, hubs hiring sub-seniors; ~50 = moderate demand, not specially privileged, picky market; ~20 = small/saturated, no shortage status.

factors.label: AI / ML specialisation depth
factors.description: Assess the AI/ML ecosystem specifically: national AI strategy/funding, AI roles on bottleneck lists, density of AI labs/employers, Stanford AI Index talent/investment. Anchors: ~90 = top-tier AI hub absorbing two AI engineers; ~50 = growing AI sector in 1-2 cities; ~20 = thin AI scene.

factors.label: Software / AI salary levels (PPP-aware)
factors.description: Median and entry-to-mid software & AI salaries from national-statistics/OECD wage data plus reputable surveys, PPP-aware; flag whether pay clears visa salary thresholds. Gross pay scale only (cost-of-living lives in income-cost). Anchors: ~90 = high absolute+PPP pay with AI premium; ~50 = European-average tech pay; ~20 = low relative to peers.

factors.label: Foreign-graduate & BD-applicant real hireability
factors.description: BD-lens real hireability beyond legal demand: language-gating of roles, employer attitudes to non-EU/foreign hires, recognition of foreign degrees/online experience, international-graduate stay/retention rates, OECD migrant employment gap. Anchors: ~90 = English-workable, strong graduate retention; ~50 = English roles mainly in hubs/MNCs; ~20 = local-language-gated, weak retention.

factors.label: Market capacity for two tech earners
factors.description: Market capacity to absorb BOTH spouses in tech (owns market placement of two earners; legal right-to-work lives in spouse-family). Breadth of employers/cities so both find roles. Anchors: ~90 = deep multi-hub market; ~50 = limited to 1-2 hubs; ~20 = thin, one spouse likely underplaced.

name: Visa Accessibility (Bangladesh)
description: Bangladeshi-national lens: acceptance vs refusal, Dhaka mission, processing, financial bars, diplomatic ties, bans.

factors.label: BD student-visa approval vs refusal rate
factors.description: Bangladeshi-national student-visa approval vs refusal rates from official/embassy/ICEF data, current year. Anchors: ~90 = high BD approval, low refusal; ~50 = mixed/moderate refusal; ~20 = majority refusal or sharply rising refusals for BD.

factors.label: Dhaka mission & appointment capacity
factors.description: Whether the country processes visas in Dhaka (vs travel to New Delhi/elsewhere) and current appointment wait/capacity. Anchors: ~90 = full mission in Dhaka, short waits; ~50 = VFS/limited or moderate waits; ~20 = no in-country processing, long backlog, or paused capacity.

factors.label: Processing time, complexity & fast-track schemes
factors.description: Processing time, complexity, and any fast-track/priority study schemes available to BD applicants. Anchors: ~90 = fast, simple, priority scheme; ~50 = standard multi-week; ~20 = slow, complex, no streamlining.

factors.label: Financial-proof / blocked-account bars
factors.description: Regulatory proof-of-funds / blocked-account requirements as a barrier for a BD applicant (owns the regulatory funds threshold; living-cost affordability lives in income-cost). Anchors: ~90 = modest, flexible funds proof; ~50 = significant blocked-account/GIC; ~20 = very high upfront funds bar.

factors.label: Baseline document & genuine-student scrutiny
factors.description: Standard (baseline) document and genuine-student scrutiny burden for BD applicants. Anchors: ~90 = light, predictable documentation; ~50 = moderate scrutiny; ~20 = heavy interviews/credibility checks as standard.

factors.label: Above-baseline / nationality-targeted scrutiny & ban risk
factors.description: Above-baseline, nationality-targeted scrutiny, bans, or restriction trend for Bangladesh specifically (distinct from baseline credibility). Anchors: ~90 = none, stable ties; ~50 = some enhanced-scrutiny listing; ~20 = active ban/visa pause/heightened targeting of BD.

name: Citizenship & Passport Strength
description: Years to citizenship, dual citizenship, requirements, passport strength.

factors.label: Years to citizenship
factors.description: Years of residence/PR before naturalisation eligibility (faster is better for the goal). Anchors: ~90 = <=3-4 years; ~50 = ~6-8 years; ~20 = >=10 years.

factors.label: Naturalisation requirements
factors.description: Language level, civics test, residence/physical-presence, income/character conditions for naturalisation. Anchors: ~90 = modest language + simple test; ~50 = B1-B2 + civics; ~20 = high language (B2+/C1) plus onerous conditions.

factors.label: Dual-citizenship retention (BD lens)
factors.description: Can a naturalising Bangladeshi RETAIN BD nationality (destination permits dual AND Bangladesh permits retention via Dual Nationality Certificate)? Owns the dual question. Anchors: ~90 = dual freely allowed both sides; ~50 = allowed with formalities/exceptions; 0-15 = renunciation required or BD not on the DNC list (mark as a blocker con).

factors.label: Real-world attainability
factors.description: Real-world attainability of reaching citizenship for this profile, given upstream PR delivery, discretion, and queues. Anchors: ~90 = near-automatic once PR held; ~50 = attainable with effort; ~20 = discretionary/uncertain even after PR.

factors.label: Citizenship-stage policy stability
factors.description: Recent rule changes to the CITIZENSHIP stage only (residency/dual rules). The macro cross-stage trend lives in politics. Anchors: ~90 = stable/liberalising; ~50 = minor churn; ~20 = recent tightening (e.g. residency requirement doubled).

factors.label: Passport strength
factors.description: Henley 2026 visa-free count/rank of the country's passport (relevant only after naturalisation). Anchors: ~90 = top-10; ~50 = ~25th; ~20 = weak.

name: Post-Study Work Permit
description: PSW duration, eligibility, job-search window, transition to skilled work, spouse rights.

factors.label: PSW duration / job-search runway
factors.description: Length of the post-study work/job-search runway to find skilled work. Anchors: ~90 = >=3 years open; ~50 = ~1 year; ~20 = none or a few months.

factors.label: PSW eligibility openness (BD grad)
factors.description: Ease/openness of PSW eligibility for a Bangladeshi master's graduate (degree level/length, application window). Anchors: ~90 = automatic, generous window; ~50 = conditions/short window; ~20 = restrictive or excluded.

factors.label: Transition to skilled-work visa
factors.description: How reliably the PSW converts to a long-term skilled-work visa (deterministic offer-based vs lottery, thresholds). Anchors: ~90 = deterministic offer-based conversion; ~50 = thresholds/some friction; ~20 = lottery-gated/uncertain.

factors.label: Work freedom during PSW
factors.description: Freedom during PSW: any employer/role, full-time, self-employment allowed. Anchors: ~90 = fully open; ~50 = some restrictions; ~20 = employer-tied/limited.

factors.label: Dual-graduate spouse PSW
factors.description: Where BOTH spouses study, can each independently hold their own PSW (graduate-to-graduate case; the accompanying-dependant spouse case lives in spouse-family). Anchors: ~90 = each gets own PSW; ~50 = one strong, other limited; ~20 = principal only.

factors.label: PSW-stage policy stability
factors.description: Recent changes to the PSW stage specifically. Anchors: ~90 = stable; ~50 = minor changes; ~20 = recent cuts.

name: Spouse Work & Family
description: Dependent visa, spouse work rights during/after study, family reunification, schooling.

factors.label: Spouse legal work rights
factors.description: Legal right of the accompanying spouse to work during study and during the principal's work phase (owns the legal/visa right to work; market placement lives in job-market dual-earner-depth; own PSW permit lives in post-study-work). Anchors: ~90 = unrestricted spouse work both phases; ~50 = work allowed with conditions/hours; ~20 = no spouse work (e.g. F-2/F-3).

factors.label: Dependant-visa access (BD principal)
factors.description: Availability/ease of a dependant visa for spouse and children for a Bangladeshi principal (income thresholds, who qualifies). Anchors: ~90 = easy, low threshold; ~50 = threshold/conditions; ~20 = hard/high salary floor or unavailable.

factors.label: Family reunification & children's schooling
factors.description: Family reunification rights plus children's schooling access and cost. Anchors: ~90 = full reunification, free schooling; ~50 = some limits/cost; ~20 = restricted.

factors.label: Spouse path to independent status
factors.description: Path for the spouse to their own independent status (own work/study permit, own PR track) rather than remaining a dependant. Anchors: ~90 = clear independent route; ~50 = possible with effort; ~20 = dependant-only, no independence.

name: Master's Study Access
description: English-taught programs, cost, scholarships, duration, student work rights, spouse accompany.

factors.label: Tuition affordability & funding
factors.description: Tuition cost plus scholarship/assistantship availability for a Bangladeshi MSc applicant (owns tuition + scholarships; living cost lives in income-cost). Anchors: ~90 = low/free tuition or strong funding; ~50 = moderate tuition, some scholarships; ~20 = high tuition, little funding.

factors.label: English-taught CS/AI program depth
factors.description: Breadth and quality of English-taught CS/AI master's programmes. Anchors: ~90 = wide top-ranked English catalogue; ~50 = some English programmes; ~20 = mostly local-language.

factors.label: Admission & credential access
factors.description: Admissibility for a BD BSc-CSE applicant: degree recognition, GPA/test bars, English-proof requirements. Anchors: ~90 = directly admissible, simple; ~50 = meets bar with effort; ~20 = recognition/credential hurdles.

factors.label: Student part-time work rights
factors.description: Student part-time work allowance (hours/week) during study. Anchors: ~90 = >=20 hrs/week; ~50 = ~15-20; ~20 = minimal/none.

factors.label: Study-programme fit to PSW minimum
factors.description: Whether typical programme attributes (degree level/length) meet the country's PSW-eligibility minimum (study-programme attributes only; downstream PSW/PR are scored in their own categories). Anchors: ~90 = a standard MSc qualifies for max PSW; ~50 = some programmes qualify; ~20 = common programmes miss the threshold.

name: Permanent Residency Pathway
description: Years to PR, clarity of route, criteria difficulty, policy predictability.

factors.label: Years to PR eligibility
factors.description: Years from study/work start to PR eligibility. Anchors: ~90 = <=3-4 years; ~50 = ~5-6; ~20 = >=8 or no realistic route.

factors.label: Route clarity & accessibility
factors.description: Clarity and accessibility of the PR route for this profile. Anchors: ~90 = clear points/route, attainable; ~50 = workable but complex; ~20 = opaque/discretionary.

factors.label: Eligibility bars / criteria difficulty
factors.description: Difficulty of PR criteria (points cut-offs, salary floors, occupation lists) for two engineers. Anchors: ~90 = easily met; ~50 = competitive; ~20 = high bars/low odds.

factors.label: Language / integration requirement
factors.description: Language and integration requirement for PR specifically. Anchors: ~90 = none/low; ~50 = B1; ~20 = B2+/integration exam.

factors.label: Status continuity / retention risk
factors.description: Risk of losing status or breaking continuity before PR (probability of status loss to rule change; enforcement conduct lives in safety-law). Anchors: ~90 = secure continuity; ~50 = some risk; ~20 = fragile/at-risk.

factors.label: PR policy predictability
factors.description: Predictability/stability of the PR policy itself. Anchors: ~90 = stable; ~50 = some churn; ~20 = volatile cuts.

name: Income & Cost of Living
description: Net salary vs cost, savings potential, housing availability.

factors.label: Household net surplus
factors.description: Net monthly surplus after tax and living costs for a dual-engineer household (owns the resulting cash; tax-system mechanics live in tax). Anchors: ~90 = large surplus; ~50 = comfortable; ~20 = little left after costs.

factors.label: Housing cost & availability
factors.description: Housing cost and availability in the relevant tech cities. Anchors: ~90 = affordable and available; ~50 = pricey hubs; ~20 = severe squeeze.

factors.label: Savings potential & remittance
factors.description: Savings potential and ability to remit funds to Bangladesh. Anchors: ~90 = strong savings + easy remittance; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = minimal.

factors.label: Study-phase living affordability
factors.description: Living affordability during the study phase (living + relocation cash; tuition lives in msc-study; regulatory proof-of-funds lives in visa-access). Anchors: ~90 = manageable on student work/savings; ~50 = tight; ~20 = unaffordable without large reserves.

factors.label: Upfront relocation cash
factors.description: Upfront relocation cash needed (flights, deposits, initial setup), excluding the visa proof-of-funds figure. Anchors: ~90 = low; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = high.

name: Healthcare & Welfare
description: Healthcare access/quality/cost & coverage, unemployment support, parental benefits, pensions.

factors.label: Newcomer eligibility / waiting barrier
factors.description: When newcomers (students/new workers) become eligible for public healthcare and any waiting period or barrier. Anchors: ~90 = immediate coverage; ~50 = short wait or mandatory insurance; ~20 = long exclusion or expensive-private-only.

factors.label: Healthcare quality & access
factors.description: Healthcare quality and access (treatment waits, GP availability). Anchors: ~90 = high quality, short waits; ~50 = good but waits; ~20 = poor access/long waits.

factors.label: Cost & coverage by immigration status
factors.description: Out-of-pocket cost and coverage by immigration status (student vs worker vs PR). Anchors: ~90 = low cost, broad coverage; ~50 = co-pays/insurance; ~20 = high out-of-pocket.

factors.label: Safety net & parental cash benefits
factors.description: Unemployment support, pensions, and parental/maternity CASH benefits (owns parental/maternity cash; childcare access lives in family-childcare-benefits). Anchors: ~90 = strong net; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = weak.

factors.label: Childcare access & cost
factors.description: Childcare access and cost (childcare only; parental cash benefits live in the safety-net factor). Anchors: ~90 = subsidised, available; ~50 = costly/limited; ~20 = scarce/expensive.

name: Culture & Language
description: English usability, local-language requirements, integration, work-life balance, livability.

factors.label: English usability day-to-day
factors.description: How far English works day-to-day for work, services and daily life. Anchors: ~90 = English fully usable; ~50 = English in hubs/work but local needed for daily life; ~20 = local language essential.

factors.label: Local-language burden for status
factors.description: Local-language level required for PR/citizenship and the effort to reach it (owns language-for-status; PR/citizenship language thresholds reference here). Anchors: ~90 = none/low; ~50 = B1; ~20 = B2+/C1.

factors.label: Integration support
factors.description: Integration programmes and newcomer support. Anchors: ~90 = strong funded support; ~50 = some; ~20 = little.

factors.label: Social acceptance of South-Asian newcomers
factors.description: General social acceptance/attitude toward South-Asian newcomers (owns the general attitude; hate-crime lives in safety-law, religious sentiment in muslim-diaspora, state targeting in politics). Anchors: ~90 = welcoming/diverse; ~50 = mixed; ~20 = cool/hostile attitudes.

factors.label: Work-life balance & livability
factors.description: Work-life balance, statutory leave, and general livability/climate. Anchors: ~90 = excellent balance and livability; ~50 = average; ~20 = long-hours culture or harsh conditions.

factors.label: Cultural distance & adaptation
factors.description: Cultural distance and ease of adaptation for a Bangladeshi couple. Anchors: ~90 = easy adaptation; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = large distance/hard.

name: Safety & Rule of Law
description: Crime, rule of law, treatment of immigrants, judicial fairness.

factors.label: Personal crime & safety
factors.description: Personal crime and safety levels (Global Peace Index, crime indices). Anchors: ~90 = very safe; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = high crime.

factors.label: Lived discrimination & hate crime
factors.description: Lived discrimination and hate crime against migrants/minorities (owns hate-crime/lived bias; general attitude lives in culture-language). Anchors: ~90 = rare; ~50 = some incidents; ~20 = frequent/rising.

factors.label: Rule of law, corruption & civil justice
factors.description: Rule of law, corruption levels, and civil-justice quality (WJP Rule of Law Index). Anchors: ~90 = top-tier; ~50 = mid; ~20 = weak.

factors.label: Women's safety & GBV environment
factors.description: Women's safety and the gender-based-violence environment (relevant to the wife's day-to-day safety). Anchors: ~90 = high women's safety; ~50 = moderate; ~20 = poor.

factors.label: Immigration enforcement & due process
factors.description: Quality and fairness of immigration enforcement conduct and due process (enforcement conduct only; probability of status loss lives in pr-pathway/politics). Anchors: ~90 = fair, due process; ~50 = mixed; ~20 = harsh/arbitrary enforcement.

factors.label: Anti-discrimination law & enforcement
factors.description: Strength and actual enforcement of anti-discrimination law. Anchors: ~90 = strong and enforced; ~50 = exists but weak enforcement; ~20 = little protection.

name: Political Stability
description: Government stability, immigration-policy volatility, anti-immigrant sentiment trend.

factors.label: Immigration-policy volatility (macro)
factors.description: Macro volatility of immigration policy across stages (owns the cross-stage trend; per-stage rule changes live in their own stages). Anchors: ~90 = stable/predictable; ~50 = some churn; ~20 = highly volatile/cuts.

factors.label: Direction toward non-EU skilled migrants
factors.description: Direction of policy specifically toward non-EU skilled migrants (opening vs closing). Anchors: ~90 = actively welcoming skilled non-EU; ~50 = neutral; ~20 = closing/restricting.

factors.label: Anti-immigration electoral power
factors.description: Electoral strength of anti-immigration parties/coalitions. Anchors: ~90 = marginal; ~50 = significant minority; ~20 = governing/strong.

factors.label: Public-opinion sentiment trend
factors.description: Public-opinion trend on immigration (direction of sentiment). Anchors: ~90 = positive/stable; ~50 = mixed; ~20 = hardening negative.

factors.label: Institutional / governance stability
factors.description: Government and institutional stability. Anchors: ~90 = very stable; ~50 = some instability; ~20 = crisis/turmoil.

factors.label: State targeting of South-Asian/Muslim migrants
factors.description: State/electoral targeting of South-Asian/Muslim migrants specifically (state-level targeting only; lived hate-crime lives in safety-law, day-to-day sentiment in muslim-diaspora/culture-language). Anchors: ~90 = none; ~50 = rhetoric; ~20 = policy targeting.

name: Tax Burden
description: Income tax burden, treatment of students/foreign workers, take-home pay.

factors.label: Effective tax / social wedge
factors.description: Effective tax and social-contribution wedge on tech salaries (OECD tax wedge plus marginal rates) - system mechanics only; the resulting cash lives in income-cost. Anchors: ~90 = low wedge (<25%); ~50 = ~32%; ~20 = high (>45%).

factors.label: Expat / foreign-worker tax relief
factors.description: Special tax reliefs/regimes for inbound foreign or skilled workers (e.g. 30-percent-style rulings, expat schemes). Anchors: ~90 = generous time-limited relief; ~50 = minor; ~20 = none.

factors.label: Dual-earner tax treatment (system)
factors.description: How the tax SYSTEM treats two earners (individual vs joint assessment, brackets) - system treatment only, not the cash. Anchors: ~90 = favourable for dual earners; ~50 = neutral; ~20 = penalises the second earner.

factors.label: Study-phase tax exposure
factors.description: Tax exposure during the study phase (on stipends and part-time work). Anchors: ~90 = exempt/low; ~50 = standard; ~20 = high.

factors.label: BD tax treaty & repatriation
factors.description: Bangladesh double-taxation treaty status and ease of repatriating funds. Anchors: ~90 = treaty plus easy repatriation; ~50 = partial; ~20 = none/double-taxed.

name: Muslim Diaspora
description: Muslim & South-Asian community, halal food, mosques, religious accommodation, sentiment trend.

factors.label: Bangladeshi community presence
factors.description: Size and establishment of the Bangladeshi community specifically (owns Bengali-specific presence; broader South-Asian presence lives in the ecosystem factor; acceptance lives in culture-language). Anchors: ~90 = large established BD community; ~50 = modest; ~20 = negligible.

factors.label: Mosques & halal infrastructure
factors.description: Mosques, prayer facilities, and halal food availability/certification. Anchors: ~90 = abundant halal and mosques; ~50 = available in cities; ~20 = scarce.

factors.label: Anti-Muslim sentiment & safety trend
factors.description: Day-to-day anti-Muslim sentiment and safety trend (religious sentiment; lived hate-crime lives in safety-law, state targeting in politics). Anchors: ~90 = welcoming/safe; ~50 = mixed; ~20 = rising hostility.

factors.label: Legal religious accommodation
factors.description: Legal religious accommodation (workplace prayer, attire, halal provision, holidays). Anchors: ~90 = strong accommodation; ~50 = partial; ~20 = restrictive (e.g. attire bans).

factors.label: Broader South-Asian cultural ecosystem
factors.description: Broader South-Asian cultural ecosystem (groceries, community organisations, cultural events) beyond the BD-specific community. Anchors: ~90 = rich ecosystem; ~50 = some; ~20 = sparse.

name: Direct Work-Visa Route (Bangladesh)
description: Bangladeshi lens: can a Dhaka-based engineer be hired directly from overseas on a skilled-work visa and migrate that way — the alternative to the study-first route. Owns the entry-route / employer-sponsorship signal (moved out of job-market).

factors.label: Skilled-work visa accessibility (BD national)
factors.description: Legal/regulatory accessibility of the main skilled-work visa (EU Blue Card, UK Skilled Worker, Canada Express Entry/PGWP-to-work, Australia skilled streams, Ireland Critical Skills) for a Bangladeshi software/AI engineer: eligibility, salary floor, occupation/shortage-list status, quotas. Owns the regulatory route only (employer behaviour lives in employer-sponsorship-willingness). Anchors: ~90 = open, low salary floor, IT on shortage list, no quota; ~50 = available with salary/quota friction; ~20 = high floors, quota-locked, or no realistic direct route.

factors.label: Overseas direct-hire feasibility
factors.description: Can a firm hire and relocate a candidate directly from Bangladesh with no in-country presence / local-job-offer precondition (global-talent, employer-led, intra-company routes; recognition of remote-built experience). Anchors: ~90 = routine overseas hiring + relocation from Dhaka; ~50 = possible but employer must take extra steps; ~20 = effectively requires in-country presence first.

factors.label: BD direct-work migration track record
factors.description: Established flow of Bangladeshis who actually migrated via a direct skilled-work visa (proof the route works for this nationality). Proxies allowed (BMET outbound stats, OECD migration database, destination work-permit-by-nationality where published); mark confidence in reasoning. Anchors: ~90 = large, established BD skilled-work inflow; ~50 = modest/emerging; ~20 = negligible.

factors.label: Employer sponsorship willingness & friction
factors.description: Employer behaviour (distinct from regulatory accessibility above): prevalence of sponsor licences, labour-market-test / LMIA burden, sponsorship cost and willingness to sponsor non-resident hires. Anchors: ~90 = employers routinely sponsor, low friction; ~50 = sponsorship with cost/test friction; ~20 = few sponsors, heavy test, high cost.

factors.label: Route continuity to PR & citizenship
factors.description: Whether THIS work visa is a recognised on-ramp onward to settlement (counts toward PR, leads to citizenship) versus a dead-end temporary permit. ROUTE LINKAGE ONLY — the difficulty/timeline of PR lives in pr-pathway and of citizenship in citizenship; do not re-score those here. Anchors: ~90 = work visa is a clear settlement track; ~50 = onward route exists with switches/conditions; ~20 = temporary/dead-end, no settlement linkage.

factors.label: Current openness / policy direction (2025-26)
factors.description: Is the direct skilled-work route currently open/expanding versus restricting/quota-capped right now (2025-26). Anchors: ~90 = open/expanding, actively recruiting skilled non-EU; ~50 = stable/neutral; ~20 = tightening, new caps/fees, route shrinking.
